cards.front: What is the purpose of adding a new city at the end of a MongoDB collection?
cards.back: To create a new document and append it to the end of the collection

cards.front: How do you add a new city at the end of a MongoDB collection?
cards.back: Using the push operator (+) or the insertOne() method

cards.front: What is the difference between the push operator (+) and the insertOne() method?
cards.back: The push operator (+) adds a new element to an array, while the insertOne() method inserts a new document into the collection

cards.front: What should the format of the new city document be?
cards.back: A JSON object with the required fields (e.g. name, countryCode, etc.)

cards.front: Why is it necessary to validate the data before adding it to the collection?
cards.back: To ensure data consistency and prevent errors, such as duplicate cities
